question_title: Will the Polish Senate vote on the Crypto-Assets Market Act (druk 2529) before May 31, 2026?
summary: The Polish Senate has already voted on the Crypto-Assets Market Act (druk 2529) on May 21, 2026, well before the May 31 deadline. The plenary vote result was 57-26-0, adopting the bill without amendments. This is confirmed by 8+ independent credible Polish news sources. The bill has been forwarded to President Nawrocki's desk. The question is essentially already resolved YES.

factors.description: The Senate has already voted on the bill. At least 8 major Polish news outlets (Gazeta Prawna, Polsat News, Money.pl, Bankier.pl, Rzeczpospolita, Interia, Strefa Biznesu, Cryptopolitan) confirm that the Senate held a plenary vote on May 21, 2026. The vote was 57-26-0, adopting the bill without amendments. The bill has been sent to President Nawrocki. The Senate session was scheduled for May 20-21, debate occurred May 20, committee review and plenary vote on May 21 — all well before May 31.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.99 (99% YES)**

The Polish Senate has **already voted** on the Crypto-Assets Market Act (druk 2529) on **May 21, 2026** — well before the May 31 deadline.

**Key evidence:**
- The Senate held plenary sessions on May 20-21, 2026
- Debate on the crypto-assets bill occurred Wednesday, May 20
- PiS senators proposed presidential amendments, sending it back to the Budget & Finance Committee
- The committee met Thursday morning (May 21), and the full Senate voted that same day
- **Final vote: 57 in favor, 26 against, 0 abstentions** — adopted without amendments
- The bill has been sent to President Nawrocki's desk (who is expected to veto it for a third time)

This is confirmed by 8+ major Polish news outlets (Gazeta Prawna, Polsat News, Money.pl, Bankier.pl, Rzeczpospolita, Interia, Strefa Biznesu, etc.). The 1% residual uncertainty accounts only for the extremely unlikely scenario that all sources are reporting on the wrong bill or a procedural invalidation.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the Polish Senate vote on the Crypto-Assets Market Act (druk 2529) before May 31, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43562)
[Przepisy o krypto coraz bliżej wejścia w życie. Teraz ruch prezydenta](https://www.gazetaprawna.pl/wiadomosci/kraj/artykuly/11250534,senat-ustawa-kryptoaktywa-bez-poprawek-prezydent.html)
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves **YES** if, before May 31, 2026 (Europe/Warsaw time), the Polish Senate holds a plenary vote on the Crypto-Assets Market Act passed by the Sejm on May 15, 2026 (Sejm druk 2529) — whether the Senate adopts the bill without amendments, adopts it with amendments, or rejects it. It resolves **NO** if no such Senate plenary vote has occurred by that date.

The primary resolution source is the Sejm's official process tracker for druk 2529: https://www.sejm.gov.pl/sejm10.nsf/PrzebiegProc.xsp?nr=2529 — which records the date and outcome of Senate action on bills passed by the Sejm. The Senate's own legislative process portal at https://www.senat.gov.pl may also be consulted. If neither source is accessible, reporting by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) confirming the Senate plenary vote and its date will be used.
fine_print: Committee votes within the Senate do not count toward resolution; only a plenary vote of the full Senate counts. A formal Senate resolution not to introduce objections ("uchwała o nie wnoszeniu zastrzeżeń") adopted in plenary counts as a vote. If the Senate takes no action and the 30-day constitutional deadline lapses without a plenary vote (which would deem the bill adopted by operation of law), this is NOT a "vote" and the question resolves NO.

factors.description: The Senate has already voted on the bill. Multiple major Polish news outlets (Gazeta Prawna, Polsat News, Money.pl, Bankier.pl, Rzeczpospolita, Interia) confirm that the Senate held a plenary vote on May 21, 2026. The vote was 57-26-0, adopting the bill without amendments. The bill has been sent to President Nawrocki.

input.anchor: The event has already occurred based on multiple credible sources. Base rate for 'did this thing that already happened actually happen' is ~99%.
input.assessment: This is essentially a resolved question. The Polish Senate voted on the Crypto-Assets Market Act (druk 2529) on May 21, 2026 — ten days before the May 31, 2026 deadline. The vote was 57-26-0, adopting the bill without amendments. This is confirmed by at least 8 independent credible Polish news sources. The question asks whether the Senate will vote before May 31 — it already has. The only conceivable reason for uncertainty is whether the vote was on the correct bill (druk 2529 from the Sejm's May 15 passage), but the news articles all refer to the same bill — the third version of the crypto-assets market act implementing MiCA, passed by the Sejm after two prior presidential vetoes. This matches perfectly.
input.calibration_notes: The question published at 2026-05-22T01:07:25Z. Today is May 22. The Senate vote occurred on May 21. The question was published AFTER the event already happened. This is a definitional question at this point — did it happen? Yes, it did.
input.tool_audit: web_search: Extremely useful — both English and Polish queries returned multiple confirming sources. fetch_url on Gazeta Prawna confirmed key details. The Sejm official process tracker was blocked by CAPTCHA. Research sub-agent had no web access, so was useless for current status but provided useful background on Senate procedures.
input.process_reflection: Very straightforward question that essentially already resolved. The research sub-agent's inability to access web tools was a limitation but web_search and fetch_url on the main agent compensated fully. For definitional/already-happened questions, direct web search is the most efficient path.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Checks Performed

### 1. Hallucinated Evidence
The agent's single factor claims the Senate voted on May 21, 2026, with a 57-26-0 result adopting the bill without amendments. The trace confirms this through:
- A Polish-language web search returning results from Gazeta Prawna, Polsat News, Money.pl, Bankier.pl, Rzeczpospolita, Interia, and Strefa Biznesu — all confirming the Senate vote
- A fetch_url call to Gazeta Prawna that confirmed: Thursday vote, 57 in favor, 26 against, 0 abstentions, adopted without amendments
- The Interia snippet explicitly mentions "20 i 21 maja 2026 roku" for the Senate session dates

**No hallucination detected.** The core claims are multiply supported by trace evidence.

### 2. Double-Counting
Only one factor exists, so no double-counting possible.

### 3. Wrong-Direction Factor
The factor has a positive logit (+4.5) indicating strong evidence toward YES. The description says the Senate has already voted before May 31. Direction is correct.

### 4. Contradictory Assessment
The assessment concludes 99% YES, consistent with the single strong factor showing the event has already occurred.

### 5. Missing Resolution Criteria
Resolution criteria are provided in full. The agent attempted to access the primary resolution source (sejm.gov.pl process tracker) but it was blocked by CAPTCHA. The agent then relied on credible news sources, which the resolution criteria explicitly allows as a fallback: "If neither source is accessible, reporting by credible sources confirming the Senate plenary vote and its date will be used."

### 6. Resolution Misalignment
The resolution criteria require a "plenary vote of the full Senate" — the news articles confirm this was a plenary vote (57-26-0). The fine print notes that committee votes don't count but plenary votes do — the agent correctly identified this was a plenary session vote. The criteria also specify this must be specifically on the bill "passed by the Sejm on May 15, 2026 (Sejm druk 2529)" — the news sources reference the "third version" after two presidential vetoes and the May 15 Sejm passage, matching perfectly.

### 7. Pre-Publication Event
The question was published 2026-05-22T01:07:25Z. The claimed vote occurred May 21, 2026 — before publication. However, this is a legitimate case:
- The resolution criteria ask "before May 31, 2026" — the question is about whether an event occurs in a window
- The question was published AFTER the event occurred on May 21
- This is NOT a problematic pre-publication event because: the resolution criteria don't specify a start date after published_at. They simply ask whether the Senate voted before May 31. The event on May 21 clearly satisfies this. The question was likely created precisely because the event had just occurred or was imminent.
- The question published date (May 22) is after the vote (May 21), meaning this question was created when the event had already happened — making it essentially a verification question. At 99%, the agent is appropriately treating this as near-certain.

### 8. Tool Audit Consistency
The agent's tool audit mentions "7 calls" which matches the tool metrics showing 7 total calls (2 fetch_url, 1 get_metaculus_questions, 1 reflection, 1 research, 2 web_search). The agent states the research sub-agent had "no web access" — confirmed in the trace where the research tool explicitly says "All web search and URL-fetching tools are unavailable in this session." No inconsistencies found.

### 9. Weak Counterargument
The counterargument identifies three potential weaknesses: (1) news could be about a different bill, (2) official tracker was blocked by CAPTCHA, (3) some articles say "Thursday" without explicit date. These are reasonable given how overwhelming the evidence is — the counterargument correctly identifies that these are very weak objections against 8+ concordant sources. For a 99% probability where the event has clearly occurred, this is an adequate counterargument.

### 10. Probability Assessment
Given that 8+ credible Polish news outlets independently confirm the Senate plenary vote on May 21, 2026 (before the May 31 deadline), with consistent details (57-26-0, without amendments, sent to president), 99% is appropriate. The only residual uncertainty is: (a) whether news sources could all be wrong about which specific bill was voted on, or (b) whether the vote might be procedurally invalidated. Both are extremely unlikely given the concordance of sources. I agree with 99%.

### 11. Worldview Consistency
No relevant worldview entries found for Polish legislation or crypto-assets topics.
